zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Wood於從1490年到1495年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾可利崔時，她的父親Oliver Wood33歲，母親Johanna Cantelupe30歲。她於1515年與Walter Mantell在英國英格蘭肯特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她於1521年住在英國諾薩普頓史爾。她去世於1567年9月18日在英國肯特，埋葬在英國肯特坎特布瑞。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Wood于从1490年到1495年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾可利崔时，她的父亲Oliver Wood33岁，母亲Johanna Cantelupe30岁。她于1515年与Walter Mantell在英国英格兰肯特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她于1521年住在英国諾薩普頓史爾。她去世于1567年9月18日在英国肯特，埋葬在英国肯特坎特布瑞。

nameMeanings.name: Wood
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: mainly a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a wood, from Middle English wode ‘wood’ (Old English wudu). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, such as French Bois and Polish Les .
English: in a few cases, a nickname for an eccentric or perhaps a violent person, from Middle English wode ‘frenzied, wild’ (Old English wōd).

variants.name: Woode
meanings: English: variant of   Wood  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 312, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

variants.name: Bois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Woods
meanings: English: variant of Wood with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s.

Irish: adopted as a translation of Ó Cuill ‘descendant of Coll’ (see Quill ), or in Ulster of Mac Con Coille ‘son of Cú Choille’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the wood’, which has also been mistranslated Cox , as if formed with coileach ‘cock, rooster’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Wood was born from 1490 to 1495, in Collingtree, Northamptonshire, England, her father, Oliver Wood, was 33 and her mother, Johanna Cantelupe, was 30. She married Walter Mantell in 1515, in Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 7 daughters. She lived in Northamptonshire, England in 1521. She died on 18 September 1567, in Kent, England, and was buried in Canterbury, Kent, England.

Elle a épousé Walter Mantell en 1515, à Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 7 filles. Elle a vécu à Northamptonshire, Angleterre en 1521. Elle est décédée le 18 septembre 1567, à Kent, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Canterbury, Kent, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Woodがから1490年まで1495年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアコリントレで生まれた時，父Oliver Woodは33歳，母Johanna Cantelupeは30歳であった。1515年にイギリスイギリスケントでWalter Mantellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1521年にはイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアに住んでいた。彼女は1567年9月18日にイギリスケントで死去し，イギリスケントカンテルブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Canterbury St. Augustine, Canterbury, Ke: 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 캉르버이 캉르버이 스트 오구스틴
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Wood(이)가 1490년 부터 1495년 까지 영국 노르섬프토실르 콜링트리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Oliver Wood(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Johanna Cantelupe(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1515년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트에서 Walter Mantell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 1521년도에 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 살았다. 그녀는 1567년 9월 18일 영국 켄트에서 사망했으며 영국 켄트 캉르버이에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Walter Mantell em 1515, em Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela viveu em Northamptonshire, Inglaterra em 1521. ru.Canterbury St. Augustine, Canterbury, Ke: кантирбури ст аугустаин, кантирбури, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Wood родилась с 1490 по 1495 в местности коллингтри, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Oliver Wood было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Johanna Cantelupe, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Walter Mantell в 1515 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 7 дочерей. Она жила в местности нортамптоншир, Англия в 1521 году. Она умерла 18 сентября 1567 г. в местности кэнт, Англия и была похоронена в местности кантирбури, кэнт, Англия.
